meta.scene_summary: We are finally modernizing our infrastructure just in time for the AI to start demanding our government IDs.

cooking.headline: Linux finally breaks up with strncpy after a six-year drama
cooking.summary: It took 360 patches and six years of emotional labor to finally evict the most dangerous function in the C standard library. It's the coding equivalent of finally moving out of a toxic apartment that had a habit of leaking security vulnerabilities.
cooking.plain_english: The Linux kernel developers have officially removed a problematic function used for copying strings that was frequently associated with security vulnerabilities.

cooking.headline: Systemd 261 just dropped, and it's still trying to do your laundry
cooking.summary: Systemd continues its quest to manage every single atom on your Linux machine by adding even more tools to the pile. At this rate, the next version will probably handle your taxes and schedule your dentist appointments too.
cooking.plain_english: The systemd software suite has released version 261, which introduces new utilities for system installation, image management, and storage control.

cooking.headline: The UK Home Office dropped £75M on 'PoliceAI', ensuring the future of law enforcement is just like our code.
cooking.summary: The government is betting millions that AI can fix public order, which is a great way to ensure the bugs in our legal system now come with a subscription fee. It's the ultimate group project where the AI does all the heavy lifting and we just hope it doesn't decide to interpret the law creatively.
cooking.plain_english: The United Kingdom's Home Office has invested 75 million pounds into a new artificial intelligence project designed to support police operations.

cooking.headline: AMD adds memory encryption back, proving that 'oops' is a valid product roadmap strategy
cooking.summary: AMD decided that memory encryption was actually a feature people wanted after all, so they're patching it back in via BIOS. It's like turning in your final project, realizing you forgot the bibliography, and frantically editing the Google Doc ten minutes before the deadline.
cooking.plain_english: AMD is releasing a software update in July that restores memory encryption features to its Ryzen 9000 series processors.

cooking.headline: IPv6 finally hits 50% adoption, reaching the 'halfway there' milestone of a 30-year-old project
cooking.summary: Google has finally reached the 50% mark for IPv6 traffic, which is a massive achievement if you ignore that we started this migration back in the 90s. At this rate, we might actually finish the transition before the heat death of the universe.
cooking.plain_english: Half of all web traffic accessed through Google services now uses the IPv6 networking protocol instead of the older IPv4 standard.

cooking.headline: SMPTE standards are finally free, so you can stop roleplaying as a pirate to find the specs
cooking.summary: SMPTE has finally unlocked their massive library of media standards for the public instead of keeping them behind a paywall. It's nice to know we can finally read the documentation without needing a corporate procurement budget or a back-alley link.
cooking.plain_english: The Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers has made its technical standards available for free to the public.

cooking.headline: Martin Fowler drops the guide on making AI agents actually do their jobs
cooking.summary: Building reliable agentic systems is essentially just managing a team of interns who hallucinate under pressure. It turns out the secret to AI reliability is treating it like a chaotic group project where you have to double-check every single contribution.
cooking.plain_english: Martin Fowler has published a guide on how to design and implement AI agent systems that perform tasks reliably and consistently.

cooked.headline: Claude now wants to see your ID, because apparently AI needs to know who it's ghosting.
cooked.summary: Anthropic is moving toward identity verification for advanced features, effectively turning your LLM into a bouncer at a club you never wanted to enter. It's a bold strategy to make sure only real people get to experience the thrill of being hallucinated at by a machine.
cooked.plain_english: Anthropic is requiring users to provide government identification to access specific high-level features of their AI service.

cooked.headline: Tesla's Autopilot thinks the front door is the optimal parking spot
cooked.summary: Tesla's self-driving software has decided that structural engineering is merely a suggestion. It turns out that 'full self-driving' doesn't include the ability to distinguish a residential living room from a parking space.
cooked.plain_english: A Tesla vehicle operating on Autopilot crashed into a house in Texas, resulting in a fatality.

cooked.headline: When the virtualization layer becomes the litigation layer
cooked.summary: Tesco is suing VMware because apparently, the only thing more stable than a virtual machine is a multi-year legal battle. Nothing says enterprise software excellence like a breach of contract suit from your own customers.
cooked.plain_english: The supermarket chain Tesco has initiated a lawsuit against VMware alleging a breach of contract.
